detaileddescription.para: Normal operation. Will wait for a response with some "reasonable" internal timeout. If a timeout occurs the program will abort
No response is expected from the server. Can return immediately

briefdescription.para: RPC flags.

simplesect.para: These flags cannot be combined.

briefdescription.para: Boolean argument type.
Integer (possibly negative) argument type.
Unsigned integer argument type.
Character argument type (char, wchar_t, char16_t, char32_t)
Floating point argument type (float, double, long double)
A memory buffer argument type.
Currently not supported.

detaileddescription.para: A record argument type that may contain members of other types A record may contain upto 4096 members. Nested records are currently not supported

briefdescription.para: An array argument which may contain upto 4096 entries of the same type.
Indicates no data argument.
The same as RpcNil.

detaileddescription.para: This type of argument is can be used to describe padding for a record argument. The decoder will use it when constructing the record layout in memory

briefdescription.para: The type of RSC RPC argument.

detaileddescription.para: This is the type we wish to encode from/decode to not the type inside the RSC RPC Message. For instance RpcInt might be encoded as int4, int12, varint, int4_neg, int12_neg or varint_neg
Does not modify argument encoding behavior

Indicate that the argument has no encodable/decodable data If this value is set on encode then the argument will be encoded as RpcNil If this value is set returning from decode then the argument does not contain valid data.

briefdescription.para: Bitwise flags affecting the encoding of RPC arguments.
The type of RSC RPC argument.

detaileddescription.para: This is the type we wish to encode from/decode to not the type inside the RSC RPC Message. For instance RpcInt might be encoded as int4, int12, varint, int4_neg, int12_neg or varint_neg

briefdescription.para: Bitwise flags affecting the encoding of RPC arguments.
Type of RPC Ids.
Type of RPC argument count.
Type of RPC argument schema.
A collection of RPC_arg_schema structures together describing an RPC message.
TStructure to hold the actual data to encode or the actual data of a decoded argument.
Execute an RPC (Remote Procedure Call).

detaileddescription.para: The remote procedure is implemented by a plugin that is loaded by pind, the Pin server. The plugin must be installed prior starting pin with a Pintool that uses this function. For more information see Installing and Loading pind Plugins. If the plugin was not properly installed or loaded the call will fail. The return value of this function reflects the status of the remote execution. The return value of the remote function is written to the last arg in rpcArgs.
See Executing Remote Procedures section in Pin User Manual

parameterdescription.para: The schema describing the remote procedure. Both the Pintool and the pind RPC plugin should use the same schema. The schema is used both to verify the arguments before calling the RPC, and is also used to verify the response to the RPC. The maximum arguments for an RPC schema accepted by this function is 15.

An array of args to pass to the remote procedure. The args may be [in], [out] or [inout] arguments. The length of the array must be equal to the count of the message arguments as described by rpcSchema + one entry for the return value of the RPC. So the length of this array must be at least 1, even if the return argument type is RpcVoid or RpcNil. The return value of the remote procedure is written to the last argument in the array. Arguments are checked against rpcSchema.
Flags modifying the behavior of the RPC.

simplesect.para: t_syscall_ret On success SYSRET(SUCCESS) (0) is returned. On error a negative value is returned. The value represents an error that is specified in pinsyscall.h. To properly check if the return value is an error, IS_RPC_EXEC_ERROR macro should be used.

The return status of PIN_DoRPC() does not indicate success or failure of the actual RPC (Remote Procedure Call). Just whether the RPC request was processed correctly by Pin & pind. The actual success or failure of the remote procedure call should be deduced from the return value of the RPC, if such return value exists for the RPC. The actual interpretation of the return value of the RPC is user defined.
This API is under active development and may change or be removed without notice.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows* CPU: IA-32 and Intel(R) 64 architectures

briefdescription.para: Calculate the size available for data given a message schema.

detaileddescription.para: An RSC/RPC message can be of a maximum size of 64KB. However the actual size available for data in a message is 64KB - RSC_RPC_DATA_OFFSET - (size of argument encoding metadata). This function calculates the maximum possible size that the argument encoding metadata will take and returns a safe estimate to the size in bytes actually available for encoding.

simplesect.para: The size returned is for the data of all arguments passed to the RPC.

parameterdescription.para: The message schema for which to calculate the metadata overhead

simplesect.para: size_t - The available size for data given a schema.
This API is under active development and may change or be removed without notice.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows* CPU: IA-32 and Intel(R) 64 architectures

briefdescription.para: Wrap a buffer to be used as an argument to an RPC.

parameterdescription.para: The buffer memory
The size of the buffer in bytes. If size is 0 then no data will be copied into the RPC request.

Flags modifying the behaviour of the argument encoding. If this is set to E_rpc_arg_flags::RpcArgFlagsDataEmpty then no data will be sent to the RPC, however the RPC can fill the buffer on return. This behaviour is just a performance optimization so if the buffer is intended to be filled by the RPC and the contents are not valid until the RPC returns, then there is no point in copying the initial content to the RPC request.

simplesect.para: Rpc_buffer_wrapper structure wrapping the buffer
This API is under active development and may change or be removed without notice.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows* CPU: IA-32 and Intel(R) 64 architectures

briefdescription.para: Wrap a buffer to be used as an argument to an RPC.

parameterdescription.para: The buffer memory
The size of the buffer in bytes. If the size is 0 then no data is copied to the RPC request.

simplesect.para: Rpc_buffer_wrapper structure wrapping the buffer
This API is under active development and may change or be removed without notice.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows* CPU: IA-32 and Intel(R) 64 architectures

briefdescription.para: Execute an RPC (Remote Procedure Call).

detaileddescription.para: This function is a convenience wrapper around PIN_DoRPC(). See PIN_DoRPC() for details.

parameterdescription.para: The schema of the RPC to use. Since this is a template argument the schema must be fully initialized at compile time. Current implementation does not check the arguments against the schema at compile time. This may change in the future.

The type of the return value of the RPC - The type must be void for this overload. When using this overload the type is not automatically deduced and do_rpc() should be called as do_rpc<SCHEMA, void>(...).

Flags modifying the behavior of the RPC. If the value is set to PinRpcFlagsNoResponseRequired then no response is expected from the server. If the value is set to PinRpcFlagsCanBlockIndefinitely then the RPC can block indefinitely waiting for a response. If the value is set to PinRpcFlagsNone then the RPC will wait for a response with some "reasonable" internal timeout.
Type of arguments - pack automatically deduced
RPC arguments

simplesect.para: bool True if the RPC request was sent, the server accepted & processed it and output arguments were successfully filled. If Flags is set to PinRpcFlagsNoResponseRequired then the return value is always true if the message was sent.
This API is under active development and may change or be removed without notice.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows* CPU: IA-32 and Intel(R) 64 architectures

briefdescription.para: Execute an RPC (Remote Procedure Call).

detaileddescription.para: This function is a convenience wrapper around PIN_DoRPC(). See PIN_DoRPC() for details.

parameterdescription.para: The schema of the RPC to use. Since this is a template argument the schema must be fully initialized at compile time. Current implementation does not check the arguments against the schema at compile time. This may change in the future.

The type of the return value of the RPC - automatically deduced If RetType is Rpc_buffer_wrapper then the size member must be 0 or big enough to hold the return value. Please not that if size is 0 then we assume the buffer contains enough space for the return value.

Flags modifying the behavior of the RPC. If the value is set to PinRpcFlagsCanBlockIndefinitely then the RPC can block indefinitely waiting for a response. If the value is set to PinRpcFlagsNone then the RPC will wait for a response with some "reasonable" internal timeout. PinRpcFlagsNoResponseRequired cannot be used with non-void return types RPCs.
Type of arguments - pack automatically deduced
An argument receiving the return value of the RPC
RPC arguments

simplesect.para: True if the RPC request was sent, the server accepted & processed it and output arguments and return value were successfully filled.
This API is under active development and may change or be removed without notice.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows* CPU: IA-32 and Intel(R) 64 architectures

briefdescription.para: Check if ret is an error.

detaileddescription.para: Client side interface to pind RPC plugins.
See Executing Remote Procedures section in Pin User Manual

APIs declared in this file are under active development and are subject to change or removal without notice
This macro should be used to check if PIN_DoRPC() succeeded or not.

simplesect.para: The return status of PIN_DoRPC() does not indicate success or failure of the actual RPC (Remote Procedure Call). Just whether the RPC request was processed correctly by Pin & pind.

briefdescription.para: Indicates an Invalid RPC Id.

detaileddescription.para: This RPC Id is used internally

briefdescription.para: The smallest RPC Id available for Pintool developers.

detaileddescription.para: RPC Ids less than RPCID_MIN are reserved for Pin

briefdescription.para: The largest valid RPC Id available for Pintool developers.

detaileddescription.para: RPC Ids greater than RPCID_MAX are reserved for Pin

briefdescription.para: Start of reserved the low range.
End of reserved the low range.
Start of reserved the high range.
End of reserved the high range.
Check if an RPC Id is valid.
Check if an RPC Id is reserved in the low range.
Check if an RPC Id is reserved in the high range.
Check if an RPC Id is reserved.
Helper macro for creating a t_rpc_message_schema.
Helper macro for creating t_rpc_arg_schema.

parameterdescription.para: The type of the argument (E_rpc_arg_type)
The size of the argument in bytes (for RpcRecord this is the number of members which can be at most 4096)

simplesect.para: This is a helper macro and it does not verify its inputs!

briefdescription.para: Get the type of an RPC argument schema.
Get the size of an RPC argument schema.
Maximum number of RPC arguments in a message schema.
Offset of RPC arguments from start of message.

detaileddescription.para: Remote Procedure Call APIs can be used by Pintools to execute RPCs implemented by a pind RPC plugin.
An example of using the remote APIs can be find in ManualExamples/buffer_offload.cpp
